questions.body: A beaker containing water is placed on the platform of a digital weighing machine. It reads 1100 g. A metal body of density 8g /cc and mass 200 g is suspended in water in the beaker (without touching the walls of the beaker). It is attached by a suitable string fixed to some support. Now the reading of weighing machine will be :
questions.subject: physics
questions.solution: As m = 200g, ρ = 8 g/cm³, V = m/ρ = 200/8 = 25 cm³, so volume displaced = 25 cm³, mass = V × ρ = 25 × 1 = 25 g, total reading = 1100 + 25 = 1125 g. 1 = r(√2 + r), r = 1/(√2 + 1)

questions.solution: In ABC triangle with inscribed circle, AB² = BC² + AC², (1-r)² = r² + r², (1-r)² = 2r², 1-r = √2 r

questions.body: Three containers A, B and C of equal volume contain N2, NO2 and CO2 respectively at the same temperature and pressure. The ascending order of their masses is :
questions.subject: chemistry
questions.solution: N₁ = 28g, NO₂ = 46g, CO₂ = 44g, ∴ Ascending order = A, C, B

questions.body: The half-life period of radioactive element is 14 hours. The fraction of the radioactive element that disintegrates in 56 hours is:
questions.subject: physics
questions.solution: Half-life of element = 14 hours, Time period = 56 hours, No. of half-lives = 4. N = N₀(1/2)ⁿ, N = N₀(1/2)⁴ = N₀/16 (taking N₀ as 1g) = 0.0625 g, ∴ The fraction of substance that disintegrates = 1 - 0.0625 = 0.9375 g

questions.body: Three line segments in a plane have lengths a, b and c. No two of these are parallel. If sqrt(a) + sqrt(b) = sqrt(c), then:

choices.a: can form an acute angled triangle.
choices.b: can form a right angled triangle.

questions.subject: maths
questions.solution: √a + √b = √c, squaring both side a + b + 2√ab = c......(i) [For any triangle A a + b > c] [2√ab is a positive quantity] Which contradict equation (i) Hence, no triangle formed.

questions.body: If D = a² + b² + c² where a and b are consecutive integers and c = ab, then √D is:
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution: D = a² + b² + c² and c = ab. Let a = x and b = x + 1. D = (x)² + (x + 1)² + [x (x + 1)]² = x² + x² + 2x + 1 + x²(x² + 2x + 1) = 2x² + 2x + 1 + x⁴ + 2x³ + x² = x⁴ + 2x³ + 3x² + 2x + 1 Which is always a square of odd numbers. So, √D is always an odd number.

questions.solution: Sodium hydrogen carbonate and sodium carbonate both are salts of weak acid (H₂CO₃) and strong base (NaOH). Thus their aqueous solutions are alkaline with pH higher than 7.

questions.body: In a rectangle ABCD the lengths of sides AB, BC, CD and DA are (5x + 2y + 2) cm, (x + y + 4) cm, (2x + 5y - 7) cm and (3x + 2y - 11) cm respectively. Which of the following statements is/are true?

choices.a: One of the sides of the rectangle is 15 cm long.
choices.b: Each diagonal of the rectangle is 39 cm long.
choices.c: Perimeter of the rectangle is 102 cm.
choices.d: Area of the rectangle is 560 cm²

questions.subject: biology

questions.body: If U = {X | X is a point on straight line AB}, P = {M | M is a point on ray AB}, Q = {N | N is a point on ray BA} and R = {L | L is point on segment AB}, then which of the following statements is/are true?
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
3x + 2y - 11 = x + y + 4
2x + y - 15 = 0
Using (i) & (2) x = 4, y = 7
So, length of rectangle = 5(4) + 2(7) + 2 = 36 cm.
Breadth of rectangle = 4 + 7 + 4 = 15 cm.
Area if rectangle = 15 × 36 = 540 sq. cm.
Diagonal of rectangle = sqrt(15^2 + 36^2) = 39 cm.
Perimeter of rectangle = 2(15 + 36) = 102 cm.

U = (X|X is a point on straight line AB),
P = (M|M is a point on ray AB),
Q = (N|N is a (oint on ray BA),
R = (L|L is point on segment AB),
Option A : P ∩ Q
: M is a point on ray AB ∩ N is a point on ray BA
: Point on segment AB.
So, option (A) is true.
Option B : P ∪ Q' = Q ∪ P
: (P ∩ Q)' [By Dmorgans law]
: Not a point on segment AB.
So, option (B) is false.
Option C : [P ∪ Q'] = R
: [(P ∩ Q)'] [By Dmorgans law]
: M is a Point on ray AB ∩ N is a point on ray BA.

: Point on segment AB.
So, option (C) is true.
Option D : P ∩ Q'
: Point on ray AB and not a point on ray BA.
P ∪ Q
: Not a point on ray AB and Point on ray BA.
So, option (D) is false.

questions.body: A body traveling along a straight line with a uniform acceleration has velocities 5m/s at a point A and 15 m/s at a point B respectively. If M is the midpoint of AB, then:

choices.a: the ratio of times taken by the body to cover distance MB and AM is (√5 - 1)/2
choices.b: the velocity at M is 5√5 m/s
choices.c: average velocity over AM is 5(√5 - 1)/2 m/s
choices.d: the product of the acceleration and the distance AB is 100 m²/s²

questions.subject: physics
questions.solution:
(i) Velocity at middle point will be
sqrt((u^2 + v^2)/2) = sqrt((5^2 + 15^2)/2) = 5sqrt(5) answer is (B)
(ii) v = u + at
5sqrt(5) = 5 + at₁
15 = 5sqrt(5) + at₂
t₁/t₂ = (15 - 5sqrt(5))/(5sqrt(5) - 5) = (3 - sqrt(5))/(sqrt(5) - 1)
= (3 - sqrt(5))/(sqrt(5) - 1) × (sqrt(5) + 1)/(sqrt(5) + 1) = (sqrt(5) - 1)/2 answer is (A) also
(ii) (v^2 - u^2)/2 = as
(225 - 25)/2 = as
∴ as = 100 m²/s² answer is (D) also

questions.body: Out of the following the metals, the one/s that is/are sonorous is/are:
questions.subject: chemistry
questions.solution: Na, Hg is not sonorous, since Na is very soft and Hg is liquid.
